sql_array: CREATE TABLE subscription_schedule (id INT PRIMARY KEY, job_name VARCHAR(50), schedule_time TIMESTAMP, last_run TIMESTAMP NULL);
INSERT INTO subscription_schedule (id, job_name, schedule_time, last_run) VALUES (1, 'SyncJob1', NOW(), NULL);
SELECT * FROM subscription_schedule WHERE last_run <> (SELECT MAX(last_run) FROM subscription_schedule);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscription_schedule s WHERE s.schedule_time <> (SELECT schedule_time FROM subscription_schedule WHERE id = 1);
CREATE TABLE job_metadata (job_id INT, status VARCHAR(20));
SELECT s.job_name, j.status FROM subscription_schedule s JOIN job_metadata j ON s.id = j.job_id WHERE s.schedule_time <> NOW();
CREATE TABLE new_job_schedule (job_id INT PRIMARY KEY, execution_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, expected_duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO new_job_schedule (job_id, execution_time, expected_duration) VALUES (1, NOW(), '1 hour');
SELECT * FROM new_job_schedule WHERE execution_time <> (SELECT MAX(execution_time) FROM new_job_schedule);
SELECT j.job_id FROM new_job_schedule j WHERE j.expected_duration IS NOT NULL AND j.execution_time <> (NOW() + INTERVAL '1 hour');
SELECT s.job_name, 'Job failed at ' || COALESCE(j.status, 'UNKNOWN') FROM subscription_schedule s LEFT JOIN job_metadata j ON s.id = j.job_id WHERE s.schedule_time <> '2023-01-01 00:00:00';
SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM j.expected_duration)/60) AS avg_duration FROM new_job_schedule j WHERE j.execution_time IS NOT NULL;
SELECT s.job_name FROM subscription_schedule s WHERE s.schedule_time <> 'NOW' OR s.last_run IS NULL;
SELECT sub_schedule(j.job_id, 'SyncJob1', s.schedule_time) FROM subscription_schedule s JOIN job_metadata j ON s.id <> j.job_id WHERE j.status <> 'COMPLETED';
CREATE TABLE temp_schedule (temp_id INT PRIMARY KEY, detail VARCHAR(100));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_schedule WHERE detail <> '' AND temp_id <> 0;
